title: GitHub Actions Tests workflow RED (pre-existing; not caused by 1.2.1)
evidence: gh run list: test.yml failing every run since >=2026-07-09; job log shows subprocess-invoked CLI emits 'Traceback' instead of table -> test_demo/test_regression fixture-comparison tests fail (5 failures + 6 errors) across all Python 3.9-3.13 x ubuntu/windows. Local pytest AND unittest discover pass on 3.14 (even under C locale). Root cause almost certainly environment-sensitive: subprocess CLI stdout encoding for box/CJK/RTL glyphs on CI runners, and/or brittle absolute-output fixtures.
resolution: Reviewer initially issued GO without checking CI; corrected. CI is not green. Diagnose the subprocess UnicodeEncode/encoding path and/or make the fixture tests environment-robust (force PYTHONUTF8=1 / encoding on the child, or skip-if-not-utf8). Separate IPD recommended; NOT a 1.2.1 blocker by itself (pre-existing) but must be disclosed and ideally fixed before advertising green CI.

title: Fixed S3-CI1: CLI reconfigures stdin/stdout/stderr to UTF-8 at startup
evidence: Root cause: a comment at src/vistab.py ~3727 described the needed UTF-8 stdout reconfigure but the code was never implemented; CLI relied on ambient encoding -> UnicodeEncodeError on ASCII/cp1252 stdio (POSIX C locale on Python<3.14, or Windows). Implemented the reconfigure for stdin+stdout+stderr (guarded, best-effort, reconfigure exists on 3.7+). Also added encoding=utf-8 to all test_cli subprocess calls.
resolution: Verified: 148 tests pass normally AND under emulated ascii-ambient (env -i LC_ALL=C PYTHONUTF8=0 python -m unittest discover tests/ => OK), which is the exact CI-failure scenario. Added TestNonUTF8Environment regression tests. build + twine still PASS. This should turn CI green once pushed.
